sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ κἀκεῖ κἀνθάδ ’ ὢν ἔμοιγ ’ ὁμῶς
sections.translation: You , Odysseus — do you champion him against me in this battle ?

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: ἔχθιστος ἔσται · σοὶ δὲ δρᾷν ἔξεσθ ’ ἃ χρή .
sections.translation: I do , though I did hate him , when it was honorable for me to hate .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: ὅστις σ ’, Ὀδυσσεῦ , μὴ λέγει γνώμῃ σοφὸν
sections.translation: But should you not also trample him now that he is dead ?

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original:
1375
φῦναι , τοιοῦτον ὄντα , μῶρός ἐστ ’ ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: Do not take delight , son of Atreus , in that superiority which brings no honor .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: Καὶ νῦν γε Τεύκρῳ τἀπὸ τοῦδ ’ ἀγγέλλομαι ,
sections.translation: Reverence , I tell you , is not easily practiced by the autocrat .

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: ὅσον τότ ’ ἐχθρὸς ἦν , τοσόνδ ’ εἶναι φίλος .
sections.translation: But it is easy to grant dispensations to friends when they advise well .

sections.label: Section 1352
sections.original: καὶ τὸν θανόντα τόνδε συνθάπτειν θέλω ,
sections.translation: A good man should listen to those in charge .

sections.label: Section 1353
sections.original: καὶ ξυμπονεῖν καὶ μηδὲν ἐλλείπειν , ὅσον
sections.translation: Stop ! Your power is victorious when you surrender to your friends .

sections.label: Section 1354
sections.original:
1380
χρὴ τοῖς ἀρίστοις ἀνδράσιν πονεῖν βροτούς .
sections.translation: Remember to what sort of man you show this kindness !

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: Ἄριστ ’ Ὀδυσσεῦ , πάντ ’ ἔχω σ ’ ἐπαινέσαι
sections.translation: The man was once my enemy , yes , but he was also noble .

sections.label: Section 1356
sections.original: λόγοισι · καί μ ’ ἔψευσας ἐλπίδος πολύ .
sections.translation: Why do you do this ? Why do you so respect an enemy ’ s corpse ?

sections.label: Section 1357
sections.original: τούτῳ γὰρ ὢν ἔχθιστος Ἀργείων ἀνὴρ ,
sections.translation: I yield to his excellence much more than his hostility .

sections.label: Section 1358
sections.original: μόνος παρέστης χερσὶν , οὐδ ’ ἔτλης παρὼν
sections.translation: Men who act as you do are the unstable sort in humankind .

sections.label: Section 1359
sections.original:
1385
θανόντι τῷδε ζῶν ἐφυβρίσαι μέγα ,
sections.translation: Quite the majority of men , I assure you , are friendly at one time , and bitter at another .

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: ὡς ὁ στρατηγὸς οὑπιβρόντητος μολὼν ,
sections.translation: So then , are these the type of friends that you recommend we make ?

sections.label: Section 1361
sections.original: αὐτός τε χὠ ξύναιμος , ἠθελησάτην
sections.translation: It is not my habit to recommend an inflexible spirit .

sections.label: Section 1362
sections.original: λωβητὸν αὐτὸν ὲκβαλεῖν , ταφῆς ἄτερ .
sections.translation: You will make us appear to be cowards today .

sections.label: Section 1363
sections.original: τοιγὰρ σφ ’ Ὀλύμπου τοῦδ ’ ὁ πρεςβεύων πατὴρ ,
sections.translation: On the contrary , we will be men of justice in the eyes of all the Greeks .

sections.label: Section 1364
sections.original:
1390
μνήμων τ ’ Ἐριννὺς καὶ τελεςφόρος Δίκη
sections.translation: Then do you truly urge me to allow the burying of the dead ?

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: κακοὺς κακῶς φθείρειαν , ὥςπερ ἤθελον
sections.translation: Yes , for I too shall come to that necessity .

sections.label: Section 1366
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρα λώβαις ἐκβαλεῖν ἀναξίως .
sections.translation: How true it is that in all things alike each man works for himself !

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.original: σὲ δ ’, ὦ γεραιοῦ σπέρμα Λαέρτου πατρὸς ,
sections.translation: And for whom should I work more than for myself ?

sections.label: Section 1368
sections.original: τάφου μὲν ὀκνῶ τοῦδ ’ ὲπιψαυfειν ἐᾷν ,
sections.translation: It must be called your doing then , not mine .

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original:
1395
μὴ τῷ θανόντι τοῦτο δυςχερὲς ποιῶ .
sections.translation: However you do it , in all respects you will at least prove beneficent .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: τὰ δ ’ ἄλλα καὶ ξύμπρασσε , κεἴ τινα στρατοῦ
sections.translation: In any case , be quite certain that to you I would grant a larger favor than this . To that man , however , as on earth , so below I give my hatred . But you can do what you will .

sections.label: Section 1374
sections.original:
1400
Ἀλλ ’ ἤθελον μέν ’ εἰ δὲ μή ’ στί σοι φίλον
sections.translation: Whoever denies , Odysseus , that you were born wise in judgment

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: πράσσειν τάδ ’ ἡμᾶς , εἰμ ’ ἐπαινἐσας τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: is a total fool since you have shown it just now .

sections.label: Section 1376
sections.original: Ἅλις · ἤδη γὰρ πολὺς ἐκτέταται
sections.translation: And now I announce that from this point on I am ready to be Teucer ’ s friend as much as I was once his enemy . And I would like to join in the burying of your dead and share your labors , omitting no service

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: θέσθ ’ ἐπίκαιρον · μία δ ’ ἐκ κλισίας
sections.translation: which mortals should render to their best and bravest warriors .

sections.label: Section 1381
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν ἴλη τὸν ὑπασπίδιον
sections.translation: Good Odysseus , I have only praise for your words . You have greatly belied my fears . Of all the Greeks you were his deadliest enemy , and yet you alone have stood by him with helping hand and did not come here and allow yourself in life

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: τάςδ ’ ἐπικούφιζ ’· ἔτι γὰρ θερμαὶ
sections.translation: to violate the dead Ajax ruthlessly , as did the crazed general who came , since he and his brother wanted to cast out the outraged corpse without burial . Therefore may the Father supreme on Olympus above us ,

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: κοὐδενί πώ ποτε λῴονι θνητῶν
sections.translation:
and the unforgetting Fury and Justice the Fulfiller destroy them for their wickedness with wicked deaths , just as they sought to cast this man out with unmerited , outrageous mistreatment .
But you , progeny of aged Laertes , I hesitate to permit you to touch the corpse in burial ,
